title: Quick Visualize ERD Popup from Dictionary Object
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to click an ERD icon on a dictionary object to see a quick popup visualization of related objects, so that I can understand object relationships without leaving the dictionary page or building a full ERD.
description:
Adds a 'Visualize' feature to the Data Dictionary that generates a conceptual ERD popup for any object directly from the dictionary page.

When a user clicks the ERD icon on a dictionary object:
1. A popup displays a conceptual ERD showing the primary object and all objects it references (outbound relationships only).
2. Objects that exist in the user's dictionary are shown in full color.
3. Related objects NOT in the user's dictionary are shown in a muted/gray style, making it clear they are referenced but not yet added.
4. Self-referencing relationships (object points to itself) are suppressed to keep the diagram clean.
5. An 'Add ERD View' button creates a new Object View named after the primary object, including only the objects that exist in the dictionary.

Before displaying the visualization, the system checks if the object's metadata includes the newer data type field. If missing, it triggers a metadata refresh first to ensure relationship data is complete.

Relationship detection uses the `referenceTo` array from field metadata and the `fieldDefinition` data type (e.g., 'Lookup(Account)').
acceptance_criteria: An ERD icon appears on each dictionary object
Clicking the icon opens a popup with a conceptual ERD visualization
Primary object and its outbound relationships are displayed
Objects in the user's dictionary are shown in full color
Related objects not in the dictionary are shown in gray/muted style
Self-referencing relationships are suppressed from the diagram
An 'Add ERD View' button creates a new Object View from the visualization
The new view includes only objects present in the user's dictionary
If metadata is missing newer fields, a refresh is triggered before display
Visualization shows only outbound (FROM) relationships, not inbound
ux_changes: ERD icon on each dictionary object opens a popup with a conceptual entity relationship diagram. Objects in the dictionary are colored, missing objects are gray. Self-references suppressed. 'Add ERD View' button creates a saved view.
middleware_changes: Generates the relationship graph by analyzing referenceTo arrays and fieldDefinition data types from stored metadata. Auto-refreshes metadata if newer fields are missing. Creates Object View records when user clicks 'Add ERD View'.
data_model_changes: New data type field added to the field metadata model to capture relationship information (e.g., 'Lookup(Account)'). Existing objects need a metadata refresh to populate this field.
api_changes: Uses Salesforce describe API data already stored locally. The fieldDefinition data provides the lookup relationship type.
implementation_notes: Relationship detection relies on the `referenceTo` array from the Salesforce describe API and the `fieldDefinition` data type. Objects only in the dictionary have metadata available — for objects not yet added, the system can still identify that a relationship exists from the primary object's metadata but cannot show the full reverse relationships. A new data type field was added to the model; existing objects require a metadata refresh to populate this. Self-referencing nodes are suppressed to keep the diagram readable.
comment_summary: Developer deployed initial version but diagram was empty for product owner due to missing data type field on existing objects. A metadata refresh resolved it. Product owner requested auto-refresh before visualization if fields are missing, and suppression of self-referencing nodes. Both enhancements were implemented. Developer also suggested adding a visualize button on the Object Meta Fields page.
